posts.caption:
Some things matter more than growth in pharmacy.

We see it every day with our customers.

Pharmacists finishing at 5. Owners who haven't worked a Saturday in months. Parents making it home for bedtime instead of locking up at 8pm.

And the best part? They actually get both.

Owners doing 15,000 items a month who still leave on time. Businesses scaling without the person behind them burning out.

But what drives Sajid more than anything, going pharmacy to pharmacy, seeing Titan customers up close, isn't the volume. It isn't the efficiency.

It's a pharmacist, a father of four, saying: I get to go home on time. I don't have to work weekends. I get to enjoy my family.

Business is business. But you didn't get into pharmacy to miss your life.

#CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyTech #IndependentPharmacy

posts.alt_text:
# TITAN_ValentinesAILoveStory – Alt Text

## Design Brief (from Notion)

7-slide carousel — ROMANTIC MOVIE POSTER PARODIES

CONCEPT: Each slide is a fake romantic movie poster, parodying a famous romance film with pharmacy/AI taglines. Bold cinematic typography, warm golden/rose colour grade throughout. Illustrated silhouette figures or stylised pharmacy scenes — NOT actual movie stills. Consistent layout across all slides: movie title top, tagline centre/bottom, subtle romantic poster framing (lens flare, dramatic lighting, etc.)

Slide 1: HOOK — Generic rom-com poster style
Title: "Me & AI: A Pharmacy Love Story"
Tagline: "This Valentine's Day… one pharmacist found the one."
Visual: Silhouette of pharmacist gazing at a glowing screen. Warm golden tones, cinematic lens flare.

Slide 2: "The Notebook" parody
Title: "The Logbook"
Tagline: "She checked every script by hand. Until AI said… I'll do it."
Visual: Two silhouettes in dramatic embrace pose — one holding a prescription bag. Rain optional.

Slide 3: "Titanic" parody
Title: "Titan-ic"
Tagline: "I'll never let go… of your repeat prescriptions."
Visual: Iconic bow-of-the-ship pose but with pharmacy props. Could be a pharmacist with arms spread in front of a glowing Titan screen.

Slide 4: "Crazy, Stupid, Love" parody
Title: "Crazy, Stupid, Workflow"
Tagline: "You had me at 'batch complete'."
Visual: Two figures across a pharmacy counter, romantic tension. One leaning on the counter, the other mid-dispensing.

Slide 5: "10 Things I Hate About You" parody
Title: "10 Things I Hate About My Old PMR"
Tagline: "Paper trails. Chasing. Late finishes. But then I met Titan."
Visual: Pharmacist tearing up a paper prescription list, confetti-style. Dramatic backlight.

Slide 6: "Love Actually" parody
Title: "Cleared Actually"
Tagline: "Friday 5pm. No baskets. No backlog. It's actually happening."
Visual: Pharmacist walking out the door into golden sunset light. Coat on. Bag packed. Pure freedom energy.

Slide 7: END CARD — "THE REVIEWS ARE IN" ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐
Styled like movie critic review quotes / Rotten Tomatoes style.
Warm cinematic background continues. Three real customer quotes styled as movie reviews with star ratings and first names:

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ — "Thanks to Titan PMR, I'm probably still in community pharmacy. Otherwise I would have left a long time ago." — Prab

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ — "Since the time we've had Titan, I think I've managed to become a dad as well." — Jeet

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ — "It's like a second pair of eyes." — Malia

Layout: Stack the three quotes vertically like pull-quote review cards. Each quote in italics with the customer name right-aligned beneath. Star ratings above each quote. Titan PMR logo + titanpmr.com at the bottom.

Optional: Add a "NOW SHOWING AT A PHARMACY NEAR YOU" line above the quotes to keep the movie theme alive.

DESIGN NOTES:
- Consistent warm golden/rose colour grade across all slides
- Bold cinematic serif typography for titles (think movie poster fonts)
- Taglines in lighter weight beneath
- Illustrated/silhouette style — NOT photographic, NOT stock photos
- Each slide must work as a standalone image if shared individually
- Keep text readable at mobile size
- Titan PMR logo subtle on slides 1-6, prominent on slide 7
- Slide 7 review quotes should feel like genuine movie critic pull-quotes — use quotation marks, italic text, and clean spacing

TONE: Funny, warm, self-aware. Every joke is a real Titan AI benefit in disguise. Product education wrapped in entertainment. Final slide grounds the humour in real customer proof.

posts.alt_text:
# Carousel Alt Text — £158 Million Clawback

**Carousel:** 2026-01-29-158-million-clawback-carousel  
**Posted To:** LinkedIn  
**Date:** 2026-01-29

---

## Slide 1 — Cover

Bold headline reads "£158 MILLION CLAWBACK". Subtext says "Announced 23 December. Two days before Christmas." A concerned illustrated pharmacist stands with hands on hips. Titan logo at the bottom.

---

## Slide 2 — What is clawback?

Title reads "What is clawback?". Text explains that the CPCF sets an allowed profit level and any excess is reclaimed by government. An illustrated pharmacist points to a bar chart showing "Allowed profit" and "Excess".

---

## Slide 3 — Here is the catch

Headline reads "Here is the catch". Text explains that clawback is based on the whole industry, not individual pharmacies. Illustration compares a tall "big chain" figure with a smaller independent pharmacist beside uneven bars.

---

## Slide 4 — The numbers

Slide titled "The numbers" with bullet points: £16.8m per quarter, starts January 2026, Category M and A drugs, no individual assessment, no appeals process. An illustrated pharmacist holds a clipboard, looking worried.

---

## Slide 5 — The bigger picture

Headline reads "The bigger picture". Bullet points highlight pharmacy closures, financial risk, fewer than 10,000 pharmacies remaining, owners taking no salary, and a £2.6bn funding gap. Icons show closure, falling charts, and warning symbols.

---

## Slide 6 — Olivier Picard quote

Large quote reads: "Pharmacies cannot plan, cannot invest, and are forced to dispense NHS medicines at a loss." Attribution: Olivier Picard, NPA Chair. Clean quote layout with Titan branding.

---

## Slide 7 — What this really means

Final slide titled "What this really means". Text explains that clawback is built into the contract, treats the sector as one block, and does not reflect individual pharmacy performance. Closing message emphasises why understanding this matters. Titan logo shown.

posts.caption:
Bulk nomination is undermining patient choice.

And it has created a completely unfair playing field.

Some PMR systems allow pharmacies to switch patients back in bulk, without consent and without a conversation. Patients have no idea their nomination has changed behind their backs.

Pharmacies who refuse to do this lose business.
Pharmacies who do it gain business.
That isn't fair competition. It's a broken system.

Bulk switching goes directly against patient choice.
And it punishes the pharmacies who try to do the right thing.

There are only two legitimate scenarios for a bulk nomination change:
• When a pharmacy is sold
• When two pharmacies merge
And even then, the NHS manages the change — not the PMR system.

Everywhere else, nomination should stay where it belongs:
with the patient.

If you agree this needs to stop, take a stand with us.

👉 Sign the petition: bit.ly/3YH8boA

posts.alt_text:
# Safy's Chemist – Verbatim Interview Transcript  
**Interviewee:** Asif (Pharmacist)  
**Location:** Safy's Chemist, Melton Road  
**Source:** Otter.ai transcription (cleaned, verbatim)  [oai_citation:0‡2025-12-15-safys-chemist-case-study-v1 (1)_otter_ai.txt](sediment://file_00000000a2f871f49771657e1e0fa008)

---

**Asif:**  
I'm Asif, and this is Safy's Chemist. I'm a pharmacist here. Safy himself has been serving the community in this area for about 45 years.  

We currently hit between fourteen and a half to fifteen thousand items a month. It's a busy pharmacy. It's a very busy pharmacy, although it doesn't seem it.

It's been a night and day difference between ProScript and now having Titan. Before, it was non stop. Baskets piling up. I'd perhaps do a travel job in here, then I'd run to the back in between my jobs, start checking, bagging, getting them on the shelf, and then running back in here for my next appointment. Back and forth constantly.

I have the peace of mind now to know that my staff can handle the dispensary. In fact, I rarely go in the dispensary anymore, just for a coffee every now and then. But that's it.

The staff are well trained. They know what they're doing with Titan. And it's not rocket science.

So I'm here from nine to six thirty. From a full day, which is a nine hour day, all the way down to maybe an hour and a bit at most. Eighty five percent of my time, pretty much, I can sit here, do my jobs, spend time with my patients.

A lot of our reviews will show that on Google as well. A lot of people appreciate that I'm spending time with them.

We would have between five to six near misses a month easily on ProScript. I've had two since we've had Titan, and that's in a year. And they have been human errors again.

Now I have zero worries. Honestly, it's the best thing that's happened.

I've recommended Titan to enough people for it to be genuine enough for me to say that, look, this is making a difference. It's changed our pharmacy.

One of my friends in Nottingham has just taken it on. He's delighted with it. A couple of firms on the road have taken it on since we had it. From the feedback I gather, everyone is over the moon with the fact that they can now focus on what's actually bringing in money, which is the services.

Well, if you're in this area and you're thinking about getting Titan, don't get it, stick to paper. If you're anywhere else, take the plunge. Honestly, the best thing ever.

---

**End of transcript**

• throughput drops ⏳

You cannot try harder out of this.

posts.caption:
Safy's Chemist on Melton Road has served its community for 45 years. Today, it's one of the busiest pharmacies in the area, dispensing 14,500 items every month.

A year ago, the pharmacy was averaging 5-6 near misses per month. Pharmacist Asif was spending his entire nine-hour day checking prescriptions, running back and forth between the consultation room and dispensary. Baskets piling up. Constant worry about patient safety.

Since switching to Titan, near misses have dropped to just 2 in an entire year.

The improvement is so significant that Asif jokes about inspector skepticism: "I'm worried the inspector is going to come in, look at my near misses, and say, 'You can't have only made two mistakes in a year.'"

This doesn't happen by asking people to be more careful. It happens when safety is designed into the workflow—when barcode scanning prevents wrong products from reaching patients, when the system won't let staff seal a bag if items are missing, when every item is tracked with batch numbers and expiry dates.

The numbers tell the story. But the real story is what changed: from constant worry to calm control, from chaos to confidence.

📖 Read the full case study: [link to be added]

#PharmacySafety #TitanPMR #DigitalPharmacy #PharmacyWorkflow #CommunityPharmacy #PharmacyOwner #PharmacyLife #PharmacyInnovation

2026, let's go! 🚀

We're feeling refreshed, motivated, and ready to help UK pharmacies transform like never before.

2025 was just the start. Together, we saw incredible transformations. Pharmacies scaling safely. Pharmacists stepping off the bench. Near misses dropping by 97%. Teams finding their flow.

Now, it's time to aim higher.

Here are the resolutions that will define 2026:

**Scale safely.** More items shouldn't mean more chaos. Build workflows that handle growth without breaking. The goal? Scale without stress.

**Free pharmacist time.** Get off the bench. Get into the consultation room. AI handles routine checks. You handle patient care. The goal? 80% of your time on services, not checking.

**Expand services.** Pharmacy First. Weight loss. Travel clinics. Ear care. The money is in services, not just dispensing. The goal? Services revenue that matches dispensing.

**Reduce near misses.** Safety isn't negotiable. Barcode scanning. AI checks. Real-time tracking. The goal? Near misses drop by 90%+.

**Go paperless.** No more printing. No more tokens. No more waste. Fully digital workflows from start to finish. The goal? 100% paperless by end of 2026.

We've seen pharmacies achieve all of these in 2025. Sagar at Brighton Hill scaled to 17,000 items and spends 80% of his time on services. Asif at Safy's cut near misses by 97% and freed 85% of his time. Teams across the UK went fully paperless.

It's not about working harder. It's about building smarter.

What's your pharmacy's #1 resolution for 2026? Share it below—we'd love to hear! 💡

Let's make 2026 the year your pharmacy transforms.

👉 Ready to see what's next? Visit titanpmr.com to learn more.

posts.comments: ## Comments

posts.caption:
The patient comes first. 

Stop nomination ping-pong.

The NHS Electronic Prescription Service is one of the most advanced prescribing systems in the world. It works because patient nomination is meant to be clear, deliberate, and respected.

But there is a flaw. Some systems can automatically reverse a patient's pharmacy nomination, without the patient knowing.

When that happens, prescriptions can bounce between pharmacies. Patients lose clarity about where their medicines are. Trust takes the hit first. And pharmacies playing fair are put at a disadvantage.

At scale, this creates risk for EPS itself. It was not designed for businesses to fight over nominations through automation.

We were asked to build this functionality. We refused. Not because we couldn't, but because it breaches our values around patient safety, professional standards, and fair competition.

Patient choice must mean something.

👉 Sign the petition: bit.ly/3YH8boA

#PatientChoice #EPS #NHSPharmacy #PharmacyEthics #PatientCare #CommunityPharmacy

**Speaker 1 (Nas):**  
Which number pharmacy is this now? Because I lost count.

This is number 11.

**Speaker 1 (Nas):**  
How was your implementation today?

**Speaker 2 (Yusuf):**  
Really good. Really good. We decided this time we were going to do it on a quiet day, Thomas, Saturday today, giving the staff as much time behind closed doors to be able to understand time on it without the phones ringing, without patients walking through the door.

posts.caption:
Independent Prescribing should be the future.

So why will it struggle in 2026?

Every branch is training more IPs. Every policy document points to a more clinical role. But in reality, most pharmacists still start their day the same way they did ten years ago: clearing repeats, chasing stock and firefighting the bench.

Pharmacy First showed the demand is there.
What is missing is capacity.

Training more prescribers cannot overcome a workflow that gives them no time to prescribe.

Until the operational model changes, IP cannot scale.
The bottleneck is not skill. It is time.

👉 This carousel outlines the problem
👉 Full breakdown in the blog (link in comments)

# Carousel Slides – TITAN + NHS Independent Prescribing

## Slide 1 — Title

**On-slide text:**  
TITAN + NHS Independent Prescribing: Another Step Forward!

**Alt text / description:**  
Gradient background, NHS + TITAN logos, text announcing involvement in the Independent Prescribing Pilot.

---

## Slide 2 — What's the NHS Independent Prescribing Pilot?

**On-slide text:**  
What's the NHS Independent Prescribing Pilot?

- The NHS aims for all pharmacists to be able to prescribe in the future.

- A pilot has been launched with 200 pharmacies in the UK.

- 40 of these pharmacies use TITAN!

**Alt text / description:**  
Text slide with green tick icons.

---

## Slide 3 — Who is CLEO?

**On-slide text:**  
Who is CLEO?

CLEO is an independent prescribing system chosen by the NHS for this pilot.

While TITAN and CLEO are separate systems, we've worked closely with the NHS to ensure compatibility for our users.

**Alt text / description:**  
Text slide, white background gradient.

---

## Slide 4 — What Does This Mean for Pharmacies?

**On-slide text:**  
What Does This Mean for Pharmacies?

- TITAN pharmacies in the pilot can prescribe through CLEO without disruption.

- Pharmacists can focus on patient care instead of being stuck in dispensing.

- TITAN continues to lead the way in innovation.

**Alt text / description:**  
Text slide with red pin icons.

---

## Slide 5 — What If Pharmacies Want CLEO?

**On-slide text:**  
What If Pharmacies Want CLEO?

Since the NHS Programme Team manages CLEO's rollout, pharmacies should contact their local NHS ICB for details.

TITAN is here to support our users in this new era.

**Alt text / description:**  
Text slide.

---

## Slide 6 — What's Next?

**On-slide text:**  
What's Next?

- The NHS will be in contact with pharmacies signed up for the programme.

- Keep an eye out for updates!

- TITAN will support pharmacies as they integrate prescribing into workflow.

**Alt text / description:**  
Text + rocket emoji.

---

## Slide 7 — CTA

**On-slide text:**  
Find this helpful? Like & Repost!

Follow to keep up with the industry.

**Alt text / description:**  
CTA slide, arrow pointing to TITAN circle.

posts.caption:
Can you spot the difference?

The GTIN doesn't.

Right now in UK pharmacy, different medicines are arriving with the same GTIN barcode or a GTIN that does not match the product inside.

When that happens, the scanner passes the wrong medicine as correct. Every safety layer collapses.

Pharmacists are forced to slow down, override the check, and carry the risk that should sit with manufacturers and regulators.

This is not a workflow problem. It is a patient safety problem.

The fix is simple:
• Make accurate GTIN barcodes mandatory
• Treat incorrect GTINs as labelling errors
• Protect the final safety check that keeps patients safe

If you work in pharmacy, please add your name.
This is a small action that protects every dispenser in the country.

👉 Sign the petition: bit.ly/3XNxapM

#EveryPackCounts #PharmacySafety #GTIN #CommunityPharmacy #TitanPMR

posts.alt_text:
# Video Transcript — Yusuf BMP Healthcare Case Study Video

**Video:** 2025-11-18-bmp-case-study-video  
**Campaign:** 2025-11-10-bmp-healthcare-case-study  
**Posted To:** LinkedIn  
**Date:** 2025-11-18  
**Video Type:** Longform video (case study testimonial)

---

## Transcript

**Yusuf (0:00)**  
My name is Yusuf. I'm the head of pharmacy for BNP healthcare. We have around 150 staff across all of our branches. And items wise, we are doing about 170,000 a month.

We first started with the branch we're in today, back in 2017. Now we have a nice group of 12 currently. Pharmacy, at the time, worked differently. Everyone was chasing items, but at the end of it, ultimately, the pharmacist has still got to stand there checking those prescriptions in any of our branches.

Now with Titan, they're a pharmacist who isn't working with any ATT your checking is down to between 20 and 30%. You'll find that the pharmacist is planted inside their consultation room, and that's where they should be.

I mean, services in some of our branches were basically non existent before Titan. Our services have increased in some branches by 500%. Barcode scanning is massive. If you were to walk into our pharmacy at a busy hour, you'd probably feel like you walked into the checkout at Tescos, because all you hearing is beeping, nonstop, scanning, scan, scan. And that's what I want to hear.

It's auditable. You're able to see the time, the batch number, the expiry date, individually scanned it. And that's all because it's a strict process. And because of that, there is no more guesswork when a patient comes through the door, and by simply typing in your name, you know at what stage they're at instantly.

With our previous PMR, unless it was ready completed and in a drawer, you almost had to do a bit of guesswork to figure out where that would be. And if people hadn't stuck to the process, it could have been a number of places.

Batch flow has double the speed of normal dispensing. You scan it, it tells you what basket to put it in. You affix the label, and you put it in. By making it as simple as that, it's probably made it infinitely more safer.

There is no other PMR company out there listening to everyone's suggestions and things like that. It doesn't happen. There's not one software provider that will do that in the pharmacy industry, other than Titan.

Change is always difficult for a lot of people, especially when you've been using a PMR for so long. Titan empowers the staff to be able to take control of the dispensary. They actually feel like they have more responsibility and more control over it.

We haven't reduced our staffing levels. We're actually looking to improve the capabilities of our staff.

We're not chasing items anymore. We're about service. That's what pharmacies should be. Titan allows you to do that, and I can't say the same for a lot of other PMRs.

We're not chasing items anymore. We're about service. That's what pharmacies should be. Titan allows you to do that, and I can't say the same for a lot of other PMRs.

posts.caption:
We caught up with Zeinab from PillSorted at The Pharmacy Show 2025 💬

After 20 years in pharmacy, she's seen how one small detail can protect or endanger a patient.

Her message was simple:

Barcodes save lives.

If a barcode is reused or wrong, it can lead to dangerous dispensing errors.

That's why Pillsorted is campaigning to make barcodes mandatory for every MHRA medicine application, and why Titan shares that same focus on accuracy and patient safety.

Two teams. One goal.

Zero errors reaching the patient.

Learn more and sign the petition here: barcode.pillsorted.com

Watch the conversation 👇

#PharmacyShow2025 #TitanPMR #Pillsorted #PharmacySafety #BarcodeAccuracy #PharmacyInnovation
posts.alt_text:
**Interviewer:**  
So we're here with Zainab from Pill Sorted.

**Zainab:**  
Hi, I'm Zainab. I'm the Superintendent Pharmacist of Pill Sorted. And just like Titan, we understand the importance of barcodes in all of the dispensing process — especially the patient safety part. As a pharmacist who's worked 20 years, if there's any changes to a box of a medicine, that box can be recalled by the MHRA — and that's any change other than the barcode. But barcodes are becoming more and more important in the whole process, so any error in that can cause significant risk to the patients. That's what we're very passionate about. We have created a petition — I'd appreciate everybody to read and share and sign it so that we could create that barcode to be part of the application form that the manufacturers send for the MHRA so it becomes a mandatory part of the application.

**Interviewer:**  
Very good point. And obviously at Titan, we understand the importance of barcode accuracy. So Zainab, what's your opinion on Pill Sorted collaboration?

**Zainab:**  
So I've done an article in the C&D exactly about this part, because I view Titan as one of the biggest pioneers of doing pharmacy in a different way — especially barcode. And now more and more patients are understanding the importance of the barcodes as well. Let me give you an example: If a barcode on a medicine of Visor Prolog 2.5 (which is a hard medicine) gets used again on another medicine of Biosolue 10 milligram, that could cause significant danger to that patient. Titan being a pioneer, Pill Sorted being a pioneer, we can capture those errors before it reaches the patient. And we are doing important work with the NHSD and MHRA to implement this at the source — the application form that manufacturers send to the MHRA.

**Interviewer:**  
So we have two software teams ensuring barcode accuracy and patient safety. Zainab, thank you very much for your time.

**Zainab:**  
Thank you.
posts.comments:
**Mohammad Mohaghegh** — Co-Founder @ PillSorted  
Learn more and sign the petition here: barcode.pillsorted.com — Make Medicine Barcodes Accurate. A patient-safety campaign by pharmacists, for pharmacists. Barcodes are meant to protect patients — yet too many packs reach pharmacies with wrong, missing, or duplicated codes. We're calling for the Government to make accurate GTIN barcodes mandatory.

posts.caption:
We spoke to Prab at 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗣𝗵𝗮𝗿𝗺𝗮𝗰𝘆 𝗦𝗵𝗼𝘄 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟱, Titan's second ever customer.

He's seen it all.

The manual checks. The chaos. The overtime.

Now?

AI clears 80% of scripts.

Batch flow and robotics run the rest.

And he finally gets to focus on patients again.

"I'd probably have left community pharmacy by now if it wasn't for Titan."

Watch the full conversation 👇

#PharmacyShow2025 #TitanPMR #PharmacyTechnology #PharmacyInnovation
posts.alt_text:
**Interviewer:**  
So we're here with Prab, who was our second Titan customer ever. What an honor. So we're going to be asking you a few questions today. Lovely to meet you.

**Prab:**  
Nice to see you. Nice to see you again.

**Interviewer:**  
So what first made you switch to Titan?

**Prab:**  
I'd obviously worked with the Titan team before on their previous products, but I was really looking for a new way of working in my pharmacy, something that would allow me to scale, and I felt my previous PMR was not allowing me to do that. I really loved the scanning function, the safety that it brought. I totally eradicated the look alike, sound alike errors that occur traditionally in pharmacy. And I've just been on the journey ever since really and I've been really enjoying it.

**Interviewer:**  
Do you feel that there's been a big change in your workflow since you've implemented Titan?

**Prab:**  
Huge fundamental shift. I'm no longer chained to the dispensing bench. I'm able to actually think about my business in a way that I would not have been able to previously. I'm beta testing Titanverse, which has been fantastic, really enjoying that, and it's made a huge difference to my consultations. I'm now seeing more patients than ever before, and it's really nice. I'm able to have a bit more rapport with them because we're using ambient voice technology, so the transcriptions are happening live and my customers absolutely love it. It's allowed me to diversify what I'm doing and actually enjoy being in pharmacy now. If I'd carried on in the traditional way, chained to my dispensing bench, signing off labels, I think I would have left community pharmacy by now.

**Interviewer:**  
Able to exercise your clinical skills a lot more?

**Prab:**  
Yeah, it's allowed me to work to the top of my degree. I didn't become a pharmacist to be signing off labels. I'm so much more clinical than I was before, and that's thanks to my system freeing me up. We use every single module that Titan has. I really love the new version of the AI — it's clearing 70 to 80 percent of my prescriptions and I can just get on with my day. I'm in my consultation room, seeing patients, doing the bits I actually enjoy.

**Interviewer:**  
What would you say your favourite feature of Titan is?

**Prab:**  
Batch flow. I was lucky to be one of the early testers. We also have a Meditech robot using single basket mode — it's made a huge fundamental difference. We finish efficiently, the day completes cleanly, and I can rest easy. We have a fully automated pharmacy over here.

**Interviewer:**  
And lastly, if you could describe Titan in one word, what would that be?

**Prab:**  
Revolutionary.

**Interviewer:**  
Revolutionary? Thank you so much.

**Prab:**  
Thank you.

**Interviewer:**  
Thank you.

**Prab:**  
Thank you very much. You.

posts.alt_text:
**Tariq Muhammad (0:00)**  
Those of you who remember pharmacy, and are old enough to go back as long as I have, I think you'll remember that pharmacy has changed incredibly. There was a time where actually opening a pharmacy business was simple, straightforward. All you had to do was open a pharmacy near a doctor's surgery, get an NHS contract and — Bob's your uncle — prescriptions would start flowing in. It was easy.

But what that did was create complacency. We became dependent on the NHS. Based on that, we relaxed. We didn't really focus on our image and our future. We just made hay while the sun shined. It created an image of us as a profession, and that image led to the established behaviours of the people in power and what they did to pharmacy thereafter — MPs, civil servants, the government, the bodies that negotiate with us. They effectively started working in a way to erode the dispensing model that we had.

We had an erosion of the dispensing fee and the income we had, whilst we had this dangling carrot of services and other things that we could get — but it was always a bridge too far. That's gone on and on for many years, and today most of us running a pharmacy know dispensing is not viable on its own.

But the rhetoric around services does not match the reality of implementation. We hear speeches about the value of pharmacy and how important its future is to the healthcare system. But then the implementation of the services is hard to reach, and if we are successful, there are usually cutbacks. We saw it with MURs, with NMS. We start delivering NMS and when we do too many, that's no good. We heard about Pharmacy First — £600 million going into services — and then the smoke and mirrors appear again.

We see this in technology as well. I'm the CEO of Titan. We've been trying to wait for Pharmacy First now for two years and we can't get through accreditation because the implementation does not match the rhetoric.

But — there is optimism. We now have pharmacists coming out as IPs. There is demand in the healthcare system unlike ever before. GPs cannot fulfil that demand. There is opportunity for pharmacists to step into that space and lead, but we must learn from the past.

**Advice for pharmacy owners:**

1. **Control dispensing, don't be entangled in it.** Use technology to streamline safely and efficiently so pharmacists aren't physically involved in every step.

2. **Your NHS patients are your audience.** They are your future private patients.

3. **Fix the image problem.** Our past image shaped our current reality — don't repeat that mistake.

4. **Create a new offer.** We don't need to replicate GPs — give patients choice.

5. **Earn the trust.** Regulations are not tick boxes — quality matters.

6. **We must be efficient.** Nail service delivery with slick operations and tech enabling the journey.

This year we've launched Titanverse with purpose — a complete solution only for pharmacy. AI scribes for consultations. Prescribing that flows straight back to Titan. CRM to target patients. Compliance and training. Workflow, bookings, payments, integrations — all efficient and affordable.

This talk is about setting the destination, learning from the past, creating a future we can be proud of — viable, profitable, sustainable and independent, not dependent on the NHS. If I had a strapline for pharmacy: **more dentist.**

Thank you.

**Slide 1 — Pioneering Pharmacy Patient Safety**  
A bold blue-on-white cover slide titled "Pioneering Pharmacy Patient Safety," featuring a mobile phone mock-up showing a Chemist & Druggist article about barcode accuracy being a patient safety issue.

**Slide 2 — Barcode Accuracy Sounds Technical**  
Large typography emphasising "Barcode accuracy sounds technical," with supporting quote about the real human consequences of mismatches and uncertainty in pharmacy.

**Slide 3 — When Barcodes Fail, Safety Slows**  
Headline slide with the message that missing or incorrect barcodes force pharmacists to stop and manually intervene, slowing down the process and raising risk.

**Slide 4 — This Workflow Started Here**  
Statement slide explaining the origins of scan-to-label workflow, noting pharmacists depend on the reassuring confirmation when a medicine pack is scanned.

**Slide 5 — Now the Profession Follows**  
Messaging about the wider pharmacy sector adopting scanning to print labels and check expiry dates, following Titan's lead now mirrored by other PMRs.

**Slide 6 — Safety Starts with Accuracy**  
Bold slide reinforcing that barcode accuracy may sound technical but is deeply human and one of the critical issues in healthcare safety.

**Slide 7 — Setting the Standard for Safer Dispensing**  
Final CTA slide stating Titan sets the benchmark for safer dispensing, with a demo link encouraging pharmacists to learn more.

posts.caption:
Hub and spoke. Before the law changes.

Medichem Pharmacy shows how.

Currently, groups under a single entity can already implement hub & spoke strategies.

That's exactly what Jeet did.

He built a central hub, connected his four branches with Titan P2P, and reimagined what his team could do.

The results:

✅ Dispensers 4x more efficient

✅ 45% less stockholding across branches

✅ Pharmacists free to focus on services

✅ Patients walk into calmer, safer pharmacies

"Peer to peer is a brand new tool, which Titan has enabled us to use at the minute. And I think it's amazing, because if you are part of the same group, you could technically consider sending your prescriptions to be dispensed at your other branch." – Jeet Vadodaria

From 1st October, the same opportunity opens to every independent, whatever your structure. Medichem proves it can work, safely and profitably, with patients at the centre.

👇 Will your pharmacy be ready?

📖 Full case study: bit.ly/481Gih1

#PharmacyLeadership #TitanPMR #PharmacyWorkflow #HubAndSpoke #CommunityPharmacy #IndependentPharmacy #PharmacyFirst #DigitalPharmacy #AIinPharmacy #FutureOfPharmacy
posts.alt_text:
# Transcript — Medichem Hub and Spoke Longform Case Study Video

**Video:** 2025-09-26-medichem-longform-video  
**Campaign:** 2025-09-25-medichem-hub-and-spoke-case-study  
**Posted To:** LinkedIn  
**Date:** 2025-09-26

---

**Speaker 1 (VO):**  
Meet Jeet. Truth is, he runs his pharmacy in a very different way. We started in 2021.

**Speaker 2 (Jeet Vadodaria):**  
We've now got four under our belt. We'd probably be hitting about 40,000 items. And I had to make a decision — how are we going to be more efficient so that we're more profitable as a business?

**Speaker 1 (VO):**  
The idea was simple — move dispensing out of the branches, free them up to focus on care.

**Speaker 2 (Jeet Vadodaria):**  
And this is where the creation of the hub came in. The pharmacists in the branch do their own clinical checks. The dispensers decide which prescriptions can be hubbed, then they send it to the hub, it comes back packaged and ready for patients to collect. It frees the team to do everything else but dispensing.

**Unknown Speaker:**  
And the results — they speak for themselves.

**Unknown Speaker:**  
Using the Power BI data analysis that Titan gives you, they were doing around 12–15 items an hour. Now it's between 40–45 items an hour. The same dispenser who worked in branch is four times more efficient. We've reduced about 45% of stock holding capacity at branch level using peer to peer and stock control. We're not overstocked — any excess is used appropriately across branches. With peer to peer functionality, if you're part of the same group, you can technically send prescriptions to any branch and have them dispense for you — and soon, once legislation changes in October — it could be other branches as well.

**Speaker 1 (VO):**  
This isn't just a new business model — it's building a more resilient community of pharmacies.

**Speaker 2 (Jeet Vadodaria):**  
If you're thinking about getting Titan, and planning for hub and spoke — get it. The numbers speak for themselves. If you want your dispensers to be four times more efficient — then that's the route you take.

---

**HubRx**  
Well done Jeet Vadodaria and Titan.  

For clarity — doing Hub & Spoke before the law change has only been permitted when all branches operate under the same legal entity. Many independents are not structured that way, which is why this legislation is a genuine game-changer.  

Groups trading as multiple entities can, from Oct 1st, choose to build their own hub (if they want to invest in staffing and automation and their geography supports it), **or** plug into a pay-as-you-go 3rd-party provider covering branches anywhere in England.  

Different models will suit different pharmacy businesses.

posts.caption:
Sure, Teslas can drive themselves.

But pharmacists cannot swap a pill.

That is where we are in 2025.

Patients are waiting up to three weeks for essential medicines.

Not because shelves are empty.

But because pharmacists are legally blocked from substituting.

The result:

👉 Patients left untreated

👉 Surgeries drowning in calls

👉 Pharmacies stuck in the firing line

The absurdity is obvious.

We trust machines to steer cars through traffic.

But not pharmacists to use their training when a drug is out of stock.

The CCA and others are pushing for substitution rights, deprescribing roles and more front line flexibility. The NHS 10 Year Plan points the same way.

The question is not whether pharmacy is ready.

It is whether the rules will catch up.

#pharmacy #NHS #shortages #patientcare #TitanPMR

# Transcript — Titan Audit Trail Demo

**Video:** 2025-09-10-audit-trail-demo  
**Campaign:** 2025-09-10-titan-audit-trail  
**Posted To:** LinkedIn  
**Date:** 2025-09-10  
**Video Type:** Demo/short-form video

---

**Speaker:**  
As you can see, Titan has a comprehensive audit trail where we can see exactly when a prescription has been created, labelled, and handed out. For this test patient item, I can see the timestamp for when it was downloaded or created, when it was labelled, and when it was handed to the patient.

By clicking the item itself, I can see who clinically checked it, whether the label was scanned, and any manual entries. I can also see who completed each action. Previously on older systems, we signed the box to confirm dispensing — now we can instantly see who dispensed it and when it was handed out.

Following processes and protocols means we can guarantee that everything required has been handed to the patient. Without Titan this would be difficult to track.

It gives the team more independence — if full packs are ready and the item shows green, we can hand it out directly. This reduces pressure on the pharmacist while they are delivering services such as travel, and prevents long waits and frustrated patients.

Overall — fantastic.

posts.caption:
💥 What if dispensing didn't eat up every hour of the day?

This is Faz, dispenser at Howletts Pharmacy in Ruislip.

And in this brilliant tutorial, she shows exactly how Titan Batch Flow works in practice:

✔ QR-coded baskets

✔ Every item scanned, every time

✔ Split packs flagged automatically

✔ Dispensing up to 15 baskets at once

The result?

Less back-and-forth.

Less stress on the pharmacist.

More time in the diary for services and patients.

Thank you Faz for walking us through it 🙌 And thank you Pritee Panchmatia, for building a pharmacy culture where digital tools free your team to shine.

👉 Read the full Howletts Pharmacy case study: bit.ly/3UMTaQq

#pharmacy #communitypharmacy #TitanPMR #pmrreimagined #pharmacylife
posts.alt_text:
# Transcript — Howletts Pharmacy Batch Flow Tutorial

**Video:** 2025-08-28-batch-flow-tutorial  
**Campaign:** 2025-08-25-howletts-pharmacy-case-study  
**Posted To:** LinkedIn  
**Date:** 2025-08-28  
**Video Type:** Tutorial/short-form video

---

**Speaker (Faz):**  
At the moment I'm doing a bit of batch dispensing because it allows me to dispense about fifteen baskets at a time.  

Each basket has a number and a name attached to it and prints a sheet as well.

This QR code shows I've got fourteen baskets. It allows me to randomly pick all the items — I don't need to do it in order.  

It tells me which basket each item belongs to, so we label it and in it goes.  

Titan generates the pick list and everything for me. I'm just popping items into the right basket.

It's easier because I can bag everything myself — I'm not waiting for the pharmacist.  

If a patient is waiting and I know everything is ready, as long as I follow SOPs and scan every item individually — even if there are two, I still scan both.

Green means ready to close.  

Red means something needs attention — might be a split, or something needing a check.

If I try to package a split pack Titan blocks it and sends it to the pharmacist side automatically.  

That means fewer baskets for her to check, which frees up our diary for more services.

I've just completed about fifteen baskets — doing single baskets and back-and-forth takes much longer.  

With batch flow I'm faster because I collect all the dispensing items in one go.

Titan recognises fridge items and prints the label for me.  

We assign fridge, delivery and bulky item QR codes to store correctly.

When the patient arrives and their name is entered, the system shows instantly that their item is in the fridge — assigned and ready.

posts.caption:
💥 Mounjaro prices are up 170% and pharmacies are feeling it!

Here's what really changed 👇

From 1 September, Mounjaro's list price jumps by up to 170%.

But the real shock isn't just the cost. Pharmacies must now:

1️⃣ Pay the higher price upfront

2️⃣ File monthly rebate reports (splitting NHS vs private)

3️⃣ Wait for retrospective rebates — still unclear in detail

For independents running on tight margins, that's a cashflow crisis.

For patients, it risks confusion, delays, and unsafe online alternatives.

That's why within days, Titan PMR launched a custom reporting suite — now used by 1,000+ pharmacies to:

✔ Generate rebate data in minutes

✔ Stay compliant without drowning in admin

✔ Keep the focus on patients, not paperwork

When global shocks land in local pharmacy…

👉 Titan moves fast.

#pharmacy #mounjaro #pmrreimagined #TitanPMR

# Transcript — Howletts Pharmacy Longform Case Study Video

**Video:** 2025-08-26-howletts-longform-video  
**Campaign:** 2025-08-25-howletts-pharmacy-case-study  
**Posted To:** LinkedIn  
**Date:** 2025-08-26

---

**Speaker 1:**  
Meet Pritee — Superintendent and pharmacy owner of Howlett's Pharmacy in Ruislip. She's what we call a strong, independent pharmacist.

**Speaker 2 (Pritee Panchmatia):**  
My name is Pritee and I'm Superintendent at Howlett's Pharmacy. Been here just over 10 years. We dispense around 6,000–6,500 items per month.

Before Titan, the bench was purely baskets waiting to be checked — as fast as we checked, more kept arriving.

Now the environment is less stressful. We get all the jobs done — plus more — because the team is free to assist with services like earwax removal, BP checks and ABPMs, while I monitor results.

Before Titan we struggled — now we hit 60–65 NMS per month and 60–70 BP checks monthly.

The two functions I love most are scanning for accuracy checks and AI. Titan AI checks 50–60 percent of prescriptions, sometimes more. It keeps the dispensary flowing while I handle clinical work for hours without stress.

I save roughly 10 hours per week on checking — possibly more. We've gone from one private consultation a month to two or three a day.

Knowing it is safe is key — removing the human-error element. In three years, only one error has reached a patient.

I've saved money on needing a second pharmacist and more dispensing help.

Technology doesn't replace people — it frees people up. It supplements processes and makes the business more efficient.

If anyone asked whether they should get Titan — I'd say 100% you've got to do it.

Most pharmacy systems were built for paperwork.

Tick boxes. Admin checks. Spreadsheets.

But here's the truth:

Clinical pharmacy needs more than admin support.

It needs digital tools that _think clinically_.

That means:

⚡ Filtering high-risk items before they reach you
⚡ Flagging interactions and dose changes in real time
⚡ Reducing the mental noise so you can focus on people
⚡ Checking every label, every time
⚡ Adapting instantly as patients change

If your system isn't doing this, it isn't clinical-ready.

Pharmacy is moving fast. The tools you choose now will decide whether you keep up — or fall behind.

posts.caption:
Sunshine. Laughter. Basically a school sports day.

Titan BBQ 25 had it all.

Once a year, we get the whole Titan crew together for BBQ 25. A day to swap the laptops for laughter, the deadlines for a bit of good-natured rivalry, and remember what it is like to be properly outside.

🥥 Coconut shy
⚽ Football speed tests
💪 Tug of war
🏃 Sack races (with some questionable technique)
🍔 And food worth queueing twice for

It had all the magic of being back on the school field, just with fewer grazed knees and much better catering.

Thank you to every single one of you for bringing the energy, the humour and the team spirit that made BBQ 25 what it was. You turned it into more than just a day out, you made it a day to remember.

Here is to BBQ 26. Same time next year. Same team.

#TitanBBQ25 #TeamTitan #SummerCelebration

I'm not glued to the screen all the time. Titan AI is doing around 70 percent of clinical checking. Our workload has reduced dramatically.

When we were getting Titan we were either going to get a refit or get Titan. I chose Titan. I didn't need a refit because Titan saved a lot of time, effort and money. If I finish at 4.30, I go home at 4.30 — and I can still log into Titan from home.

To all competitors in the area — don't get Titan. Stay paper based.

The pharmacy runs much smoother since moving to Titan. It's more efficient and more reliable.

# Comments — 2025-08-01 Titan checks every script (single image)

---

**Hooman Ghalamkari** — Chief Operations Officer for Implementations, Invatech Health Ltd  
*6mo*

Other PMRs trying to copy TITAN, mark an item as being clinically checked and then no need to be checked again for 6-12 months. What happens if a new medication is prescribed for the patient in month 2 and it interacts with the medication that has been marked as clinically checked? Surely the whole medication therapy needs to be considered and assessed. That is why Titan carries out its checks on every prescription every time, ensuring no assumptions are made and highlighting risk factors for pharmacists to consider in carrying out their clinical checks.

❤️ 5 Likes | 💬 8 Replies

**Reply — Hooman Ghalamkari** — *5mo*  
Ruth Hulton I agree its complicated especially for Superintendent Pharmacists who are ultimately responsible. There are two important concepts that Titan AI tries to address as part of its design: Trust and Control.

Trust - That high risk meds are always reviewed by pharmacists every time. You can even set the meds, scenarios (eg new patients) and groups of patients (eg MDS) that you don't want AI to be applied to.

Titan AI is based on learning from pharmacists interventions on prescriptions. Anyone who wants to use Titan AI is encouraged to understand how it works, learns and applies its learning.
You can also run Titan AI in partial mode, where the AI makes changes but a pharmacist has to approve the changes.

Control - pharmacists are in control of how long the TITAN AI is applied for, typically 2-4 hours.
After each period pharmacists have to do a statistically significant sample audit before they can run the AI again. 

Measures like the above show the sophistication that Titan AI has and this is why hundreds of Titan customers are using it and achieving efficiencies without compromising patient safety.

years after you.

AI in UK pharmacy finally got national attention.

posts.alt_text:
# Transcript — Sagar Brighton Hill Longform Case Study Video

Meet Sagar, bit of a tech guy, and the owner of Brighton Hill Pharmacy in Basingstoke, where things run smoothly and never get chaotic.

So I'm Sagar Lungiwala. I'm the owner of Brighton Hill Pharmacy. This used to be ex-Boots. Been four years now owning this pharmacy, and I've significantly grown from 4000 items when I started to 17, 18,000 items now, which is fourfold increase in terms of items.

With the Titan PMR, it absolutely helps, because we can exactly track where the prescription is, what stage of the pharmacy it is, whether we have received the prescription or not, at what part of dispensing stage it is at, or whether it's ready for collection.

When in a busy hour of the pharmacy, I'm sure my team is all calm and collected. Most prescriptions are ready for same-day collection, provided the GP surgery sends us the prescription. It just flows.

Very easy to learn. On my day one of this current pharmacy, I was able to teach my team. The whole team was feeling comfortable in terms of dispensing the prescription.

Titan has played a big role in time leverage to run other services at the pharmacy. 80% of my time goes into the services.

In the morning, first thing I walk in I do the clinical checks. Probably around 10 or 11, I can then run the services without interrupting my dispensing flow.

We're using Repeat Flow to order the prescription for some of our Nomad patients. Titan Mobile always helps us quickly find the patient on the counter and send them a text as well.

Batch Flow is another big one. It really helps us streamline the dispensing process. We can do single patient mode or multi patient mode. Two different modes into Batch Flow which helps my team dispense the prescription.

Titan has changed the whole pharmacy workflow for me. I wouldn't even consider any other PMR. It has significantly helped to run the pharmacy smoothly.

and saved 20+ hours a week per pharmacist.

Puri Pharmacy handles 18,000+ items across two branches.

Prescriptions fly out. Services keep growing.  
Staff stay calm. Patients are impressed.

👇 What would your pharmacy do with 20 hours back?

#PharmacyLeadership #TitanPMR #DigitalPharmacy #PharmacyFirst #PharmacyWorkflow #AIinPharmacy #ModernPMR #ServiceLedPharmacy
posts.alt_text:
Meet Rahul. You might recognize him from TikTok with videos such as, "What does your mucus say about you?" He's a superintendent pharmacist at Puri Pharmacy with branches in Southall and Hillingdon, part of a new generation blending frontline care and digital first leadership.

My name is Rahul Puri, and I'm the superintendent pharmacist at Puri Pharmacy in Southall and Hillingdon. We're doing approximately 18,000 items across both branches.

Two years ago, we were frantically using paper, diaries, post-it notes, prescriptions everywhere, scribbling everywhere. Now everything is clearly visible on each screen, whether it be from the inbox when you receive a prescription, all the way to the end for the handout. Everyone can see where the prescription is. No one is frantically looking for paper. We've become a paper free pharmacy.

Titan Core, Repeat Flow and Titan AI have been revolutionary in our pharmacy. We serve a wide population of patients who are on MDS, so dosette boxes, and also patients which we routinely order for. In the older, archaic systems we had to manually do this, but now with Titan Repeat Flow, we press one button and it does it for us.

Titan's SMS feature has allowed us to text patients seamlessly. When the bag is ready for a patient, it simply goes onto a shelf or a tote and a message is sent directly to the patient. Not only has this freed up so much time for our staff, they can now focus on other tasks rather than needlessly sending SMSs to patients.

Since implementing Titan in January 2025 it's freed up at least 20 hours a week for a pharmacist in the pharmacy. Now we can offer double the amount of appointments such as Pharmacy First, which lead to better patient and health outcomes and better revenue for an independent business owner.

Now, people will come to us for things outside the conventional pharmacy realm, such as ear wax removal, medical cannabis, travel vaccines, and now people are commenting, "I didn't realise a pharmacy could do this." So we're now changing our business model from being just a dispensary in a conventional pharmacy to a community health hub.

Titan has given me the freedom to lead from the front, clinically, commercially and creatively.

posts.caption:
"Reform or die."

That's the warning behind the NHS 10-year plan.

A total reset, built on prevention, digital tools, and community-first care.

Pharmacy isn't an add-on anymore.  
We're at the heart of it.

But we've heard this before.  
Big plans. Bigger expectations.  
Not always the support to match.

Swipe through and tell us how you're feeling about it. 👀

#CommunityPharmacy #NHSPlan #PrimaryCare #PharmacyFirst #PharmacyLeadership #DigitalHealth #IntegratedCare #NHSReform #HealthPolicy #FutureOfPharmacy

posts.alt_text:
# Prab — Longform Video Transcript

**Speaker 1 (0:00)**  
Meet Prab. When he's not picking up his niece, he's here in Orpington running Priory Pharmacy. And let's just say, you can call him a smooth operator.

**Speaker 2 (0:10)**  
We're doing nearly 16,000 items, and we do this without a robot, and there's no chaos. Everything's really calm and really organised. But I did not want to have any other PMR other than Titan in here when we were redesigning this pharmacy, so I made sure I had about at least five batch flow stations. They can have 30 baskets in them each, so we can work on 150 patients simultaneously.

Here, Titan AI and batch flow are my two modules that I love the most and make the biggest difference to me in the pharmacy. The AI is currently checking and clearing about 70% of the prescriptions that we receive.

Now, Mr Jones comes in, we know exactly at what stage their prescription's at, due to the Kanban boards on the Titan system. I literally put his details in, or he shows me his collection code. It brings up his details on the device and it tells me straight away where his prescription is.

One of the things I'm really proud about, and we're one of the early adopters for, is Titan stock control. In this one space alone, we worked out we can pick 60% of every single basket just standing here.

Five years ago, I would not be able to be having this conversation with you. I'd probably still be in my dispensary trying to check hundreds of baskets, being incredibly stressed. Thanks to Titan PMR, I'm probably still in community pharmacy. Otherwise, I would have left a long time ago.

I've shown people Titan in the past. I can spend a whole hour with them and not be interrupted. Titan is efficient. It's safe. And it lets us be patient centric without burning out the team or myself.

If someone was thinking about getting Titan, I would just tell them, just sign straight away. Titan has given me my work-life balance back.

---

*Transcribed by https://otter.ai*

posts.transcript:
# Transcript — Andy Batch Flow testimonial (short video)

**Video:** 2025-06-24-andy-batch-flow-testimonial-video  
**Posted To:** LinkedIn  
**Date:** 2025-06-24

---

## Transcript

**Speaker 1** 0:00  
We were on our third site really encouraged by the progress that we've made with the other two. We had the Easter period where we've, we've probably lost about 50 hours of staff per week, and we had three bank holidays in quick succession, and we're doing a 10,000 item pharmacy, and we didn't, didn't hit a road bump. It literally went as smooth as ever has been. And the staff put that down to Titan. And the introduction of Batch Flow has made a huge difference. Being able to get all the 1-2-3 item scripts and get them done with just a couple of trips around the dispensary. You can dispense a couple of 100 items with one member of staff in a morning, and it's really, really productive. This is about week three for Kyneton. Done quite a lot of prepping. We had Titan running alongside their previous PMR so that the staff could spend some time watching the onboarding videos. They all had a go at dispensing some prescriptions using the packaging feature with the clean items, and so they all had a little bit of an exposure before we decided to switch over nominations. And we switched over nominations about two weeks ago, and everything's nice and stable at the moment, which is really good. The teams still need to get a lot more experience, a lot more confidence. I think having NMS in today has been really good, because I've got two experienced Titan users who benefited from training to really kind of stretch their knowledge and bring them up to date. The prep is really important because it means that you get better value for money out of your training day. You don't want to have your trainer come in and hold hands on the basic functions. Learning to use Titan at a basic level is like learning to use a Sainsbury's self checkout. It's really intuitive. Use the onboarding videos get experienced staff in just to do the simple stuff. What you're really looking for from your trainer is to take people from knowing Titan at 70% to taking them to 95% that's where the trainer comes in. So.

## Slide 1 — Deadline warning

Slide branded with Community Pharmacy England stating that nearly half of pharmacies have not submitted their Data Security and Protection Toolkit, highlighting a submission deadline of 30 June 2025 and warning that failure results in an inadequate governance rating under NHS contractual requirements.

---

## Slide 2 — What is DSPT?

Slide explaining that the Data Security and Protection Toolkit is a mandatory NHS requirement confirming pharmacies meet information governance and data protection standards.

---

## Slide 3 — Why DSPT matters

Slide outlining why submission is important, noting it is required for NHS funding and for delivering new pharmacy services, and warning that missing the deadline results in an inadequate governance rating.

---

## Slide 4 — Who must submit

Slide explaining that all community pharmacies in England must submit the toolkit, even if they completed it the previous year, as annual resubmission is required.

---

## Slide 5 — Where to submit

Slide showing where pharmacies must complete submission online at dsptoolkit.nhs.uk, including a screenshot of the NHS Data Security and Protection Toolkit login page.

---

---

## Slide 1 — Overload acknowledgement

Slide reading "Read this post if your PMR feels like information overload," with supporting text explaining pharmacy teams are not alone and that numbers and metrics can start to feel like noise.

---

## Slide 2 — Data overload reality

Slide explaining PMR systems present many metrics at once, listing examples such as prescription volumes, queue lengths, rejections, backlogs, safety alerts, stock shortages, and service counts, emphasising that more data does not always lead to better decisions.

---

## Slide 3 — What actually matters

Slide highlighting key day-to-day measures that matter most: time to dispense safely, service output performance, label accuracy, manageable team workload, and prescription collection rates.

---

## Slide 4 — What to stop worrying about

Slide listing metrics teams should stress less about, including scripts per hour without context, every individual EPS rejection, comparisons with large chain pharmacies, and reacting to daily data fluctuations rather than long-term trends.

---

## Slide 5 — What to focus on instead

Slide advising pharmacies to set goals based on real outcomes, use dashboards teams understand, allow automation to highlight key insights, and track performance weekly instead of minute-by-minute.

---

## Slide 6 — Final message and call to action

Closing slide stating pharmacies do not need more data but better insights, and directing viewers to read a full blog post on staying focused when managing PMR data.

posts.alt_text:
# Carousel Alt Text — Pharmacy Contract Changes: What's Actually Happening (And When)

**Carousel:** 2025-04-23-pharmacy-contract-changes-carousel  
**Posted To:** LinkedIn  
**Date:** 2025-04-23

---

## Slide 1 — Cover

Cover slide titled "Pharmacy Contract Changes: What's Actually Happening (And When)" showing logos for the Department of Health & Social Care and Community Pharmacy England, introducing a simplified 2025 timeline for contract updates aimed at busy pharmacy teams.

---

## Slide 2 — April 2025 timeline

Timeline slide for April 2025 outlining payment updates, including an increased medicines margin cap, updated single activity fee, Pharmacy First consultation payments, hypertension service payment changes, contraception service fees, and simplified New Medicine Service payments.

---

## Slide 3 — May 2025 PQS

May 2025 update explaining the opening of the Pharmacy Quality Scheme (PQS) claim window, with reminders to review eligibility, prepare documentation, submit declarations, and secure aspiration funding.

---

## Slide 4 — June 2025 Pharmacy First bands

June 2025 timeline slide showing Pharmacy First monthly payment bands beginning, with payments of £500 per month for 20–29 consultations and £1,000 per month for 30+ consultations, plus requirements to qualify.

---

## Slide 5 — July 2025 PQS payments

July 2025 update indicating PQS aspiration payments being made, alongside reminders to maintain service delivery records, monitor eligibility, and prepare for future reporting.

---

## Slide 6 — Autumn 2025 negotiations

Autumn 2025 timeline highlighting the start of negotiations for the 2026/27 pharmacy funding deal and encouraging the sector to push for better support and smarter systems.

---

## Slide 7 — October 2025 requirements

October 2025 requirements slide showing new contract conditions, including mandatory ABPM services for payments, addition of emergency contraception services, expansion of New Medicine Service conditions, and restrictions on distance selling pharmacies delivering services on premises.

---

## Slide 8 — March 2026 contraception targets

March 2026 update introducing contraception consultation minimum targets, requiring pharmacies to meet monthly consultation thresholds to continue receiving Pharmacy First payments.

---

## Slide 9 — How to get ahead

Guidance slide titled "How to Get Ahead" explaining how funding, services, and expectations are changing and advising pharmacies to understand updates, prepare systems for services, and free up time for patient care.

---

## Slide 10 — Closing CTA

Closing slide encouraging viewers to like and repost the content to share with others and follow Titan to stay updated on pharmacy industry changes.

posts.alt_text:
Outdated pharmacy workflows are slowing everything down, and your PMR isn't helping. Some even have an automatic print feature which turns your prescriptions into a growing pile of paper, and they call it automation.  

The reality is slow, messy, and it doesn't work.  

So we built something better, no paper, no voxel necks, no wasted time.  

This is the Titan way, a seamless digital first workflow designed for the modern pharmacy, and here's how it works.  

Prescriptions arrive digitally and stay digital, no printing and no paperwork.  

Using Titan AI, prescriptions go through a 40 point clinical check. Dosages are automatically corrected, and only high risk items are flags to the pharmacist.  

A smart prescription board prioritizes prescriptions so the right ones are processed first.  

Dispensers pick and scan stock, no searching and no second guessing. Barcode validation ensures accuracy for correct patient, correct drug and correct strength, along with an expiry check before any label prints.  

Bagging and shelving are fully tracked. Patients get an SMS with a collection code so staff never waste time searching.  

Collection is instant, because prescriptions are found by code on shelves, in the fridge, in the CD, cupboard, no delays, no missing items.  

Pharmacists focus on care. Dispensers work efficiently, and the patient gets what they need without the weight.  

And this is just pharmacy the way it should be.  

This is the Titan way.

---

## Slide 1 — Campaign introduction

**Alt text:**  
A bright blue gradient background with the TITAN logo on the left and the Action for Humanity logo on the right. Large white text reads "Every Purchase Makes a Real Impact," followed by smaller text stating "100% of proceeds go directly to Action for Humanity's vital programs." Along the bottom is a row of black TITAN-branded merchandise, including a hoodie, beanie, cap, mug, and jacket, neatly arranged like a product showcase.

---

## Slide 2 — The pledge

**Alt text:**  
A blue gradient background with bold white text titled "Our Pledge." The text explains that every penny of profit from TITAN merchandise goes directly to Action for Humanity. Additional copy states support for emergency relief, education, healthcare, and sustainable development projects worldwide. The Action for Humanity logo appears at the bottom right, with the TITAN logo at the bottom left.

---

## Slide 3 — Deliver critical healthcare

**Alt text:**  
A white background with blue heading text reading "Deliver Critical Healthcare." Below, black text explains that £100 could help provide medical care to those in need. The image shows a female healthcare worker wearing a headscarf and stethoscope, kneeling beside a young child and smiling while offering care, conveying compassion and trust.

---

## Slide 4 — Support education for children

**Alt text:**  
A white background with blue heading text reading "Support Education for Children." Supporting text explains that £20 could supply school materials for a child for an entire year. The image shows a smiling young girl wearing a headscarf, seated at a desk with books and papers in front of her, suggesting access to education and learning opportunities.

---

## Slide 5 — Provide immediate emergency relief

**Alt text:**  
A white background with blue heading text reading "Provide Immediate Emergency Relief." Text below states that £50 could offer food and essentials to a family for a month. The image shows an adult and a young child smiling as they unpack food items from a box together on a wooden table, representing family support and basic aid.

---

## Slide 6 — Build sustainable futures

**Alt text:**  
A white background with blue heading text reading "Build Sustainable Futures." Supporting text explains that £150 could support long-term community development projects. The image shows a woman wearing a headscarf working with soil and plants outdoors, planting crops and symbolising sustainability, resilience, and long-term growth.

---

## Slide 7 — Call to action

**Alt text:**  
A blue gradient background with the TITAN and Action for Humanity logos at the top. Large white text reads "Every Purchase Helps Change Lives," followed by a call to action: "Shop now and make a difference: shop.titanpmr.com." Along the bottom, the same range of black TITAN-branded merchandise is displayed, closing the carousel with a clear invitation to support the cause.

## Video Description

A testimonial-style video featuring a pharmacist speaking inside their pharmacy about using Titan PMR to move toward a fully paperless workflow. The pharmacist explains how dispensing no longer relies on constant pharmacist intervention, allowing the wider team to work confidently and independently. They describe benefits such as digital, trackable prescriptions, double barcode validation, and reduced clutter in the dispensary. The video focuses on freeing up time for services, empowering the team, and choosing a forward-thinking system that supports a more efficient, modern pharmacy.

---

## Slide 1 — Opening question

**Alt text:**  
A blue-toned background with a blurred pharmacy interior. Large white text asks, "Does Growth feel Exciting… or Overwhelming?" Smaller supporting text explains that pharmacy is not slowing down and that feeling the strain may be a sign you are ready to evolve. An arrow and "Scroll through to see why" prompt movement. The TITAN logo appears at the bottom.

---

## Slide 2 — More prescriptions

**Alt text:**  
An illustrated scene of a pharmacist working at a dispensary bench surrounded by stacks of prescriptions and boxes on shelves behind them. Papers are piled high, suggesting rising workload. Text reads "More Prescriptions, Bigger Impact," followed by a line explaining that rising script volumes show strong demand and that the right system can turn pressure into profit.

---

## Slide 3 — Stretched workflows

**Alt text:**  
A stylised illustration of a pharmacy team member standing behind a counter with large stacks of paperwork on both sides. A speech bubble above them reads "Workflows are feeling stretched." Text at the top says "A Busier Team, A Need for Smarter Workflows," explaining that the issue is not staffing, but the need for better systems.

---

## Slide 4 — More patients

**Alt text:**  
An illustration showing a busy pharmacy counter with multiple patients and staff interacting at once. Shelves of medicines line the background. Text reads "More Patients, More Opportunities," with supporting copy stating that a busy pharmacy signals trust and that the right system keeps service smooth and stress-free.

---

## Slide 5 — Expanding services

**Alt text:**  
A calm consultation-room illustration showing a pharmacist seated at a desk speaking with a patient who is holding a phone. Certificates hang on the wall behind them. Text reads "Expanding Services, Expanding Potential," highlighting growth in NHS and private services and how the right setup makes expansion feel manageable.

---

## Slide 6 — Ready to level up

**Alt text:**  
An illustrated pharmacist stands confidently in the middle of a modern pharmacy, hands relaxed at their sides. The space around them is organised and open. Text states "These challenges don't mean you're stuck," followed by a message that the pharmacy is ready to level up and that the right system turns pressure into progress.

---

## Slide 7 — Systems must keep up

**Alt text:**  
A detailed illustration of a modern dispensary with digital screens, organised workstations, and pharmacy staff working efficiently together. Text reads "Growth is here. Systems should keep up," followed by a line explaining that smarter technology means more efficiency, less stress, and more time where it matters most.

---

## Slide 8 — Call to action

**Alt text:**  
A clean, light background with bold blue and black text reading "Any of this sound familiar?" followed by "Like & Repost to share with others who need to see this!" The TITAN logo appears in the bottom corner, closing the carousel with a clear engagement prompt.

posts.caption:
🚨 URGENT: Independent Pharmacies Are at Risk 🚨

More closures are coming. Unless we take action now!

MPs need to hear directly from you about the funding crisis threatening independent pharmacies. Every email sent increases pressure on Parliament to act.

Follow these 3 simple steps to make them listen:

🛑 Step 1: Find Your MP
📍 Use this link: https://lnkd.in/dc3W6aS

✉️ Step 2: Send an Email
📩 Use our template (see comments) or write your own

📧 Step 3: CC IPCN & Key MPs
Make sure to CC IPCN: office@ipcn.org.uk

Also, CC the following key MPs:
📌 Wes Streeting (Health Secretary): dhsc.publicenquiries@dhsc.gov.uk
📌 Rachel Reeves (Chancellor): public.enquiries@hmtreasury.gov.uk
📌 Keir Starmer (Prime Minister): keir.starmer.mp@parliament.uk

🚨 This is urgent. Every email counts. 

Find your MP, send your email and share this with other pharmacy owners.

Let's push for change!

#PharmacyCrisis #SaveCommunityPharmacy #PharmacyFunding #IndependentPharmacy #PharmacyAdvocacy
posts.alt_text:
# Supporting Independent Pharmacies — Action Needed

**Campaign:** 2025-03-07-independent-pharmacy-advocacy  
**Asset Type:** Carousel  
**Total Slides:** 6

---

## Slide 1 — Introduction

**Alt text:**  
A clean white-to-light-blue gradient background with bold blue text reading "Make Parliament Listen!" followed by "In 3 Simple Steps." Smaller black text underneath states that community pharmacies are closing, funding is stretched too thin, and patients are suffering, ending with a call to act before it is too late. A small arrow points forward, indicating the start of a step-by-step guide. The TITAN logo appears at the bottom.

---

## Slide 2 — Step 1: Find Your MP

**Alt text:**  
A minimalist slide titled "Step 1 — Find Your MP" in large blue text. Supporting text explains that your MP represents you and should hear your voice. A screenshot-style image of the UK Parliament website's "Find MPs" page is shown on the right, displaying a list of MPs with photos and names. An arrow points toward the image, and a note advises emailing both MPs if your pharmacy is in a different area from your home.

---

## Slide 3 — Step 2: Send Your Email

**Alt text:**  
A slide with the heading "Step 2 — Send Your Email" in blue. Text explains that MPs act when they see real stories from real people. On the right is a tilted image of an email document showing a drafted message about pharmacy funding pressures. Additional text instructs viewers to use the email template in the comments or write their own, and to explain how the funding crisis affects their pharmacy, staff, and patients.

---

## Slide 4 — Step 3: CC Key Ministers

**Alt text:**  
A slide titled "Step 3 — CC Key Ministers" with blue heading text. Supporting copy encourages maximising impact by copying in decision-makers. A checklist lists IPCN, the Health Secretary Wes Streeting, Prime Minister Keir Starmer, and Chancellor Rachel Reeves. A dark screenshot-style graphic shows email addresses being copied into an email CC field. Text reinforces that thousands of emails cannot be ignored.

---

## Slide 5 — Why This Matters

**Alt text:**  
A slide with the heading "Why This Matters" in blue. Below, bullet points explain the consequences of inaction: more pharmacy closures, loss of vital patient care, pharmacy jobs at risk, and increased strain on the NHS. The tone is serious and urgent, ending with a bold statement urging immediate action before it is too late.

---

## Slide 6 — Take Action Today

**Alt text:**  
A concluding slide titled "Take Action Today" in large blue text. A checklist outlines three actions: find your MP, send your email, and demand action by copying MPs. Additional lines encourage sharing the message with other pharmacists and reposting to spread awareness. The design mirrors earlier slides with a clean gradient background and the TITAN logo at the bottom.

An illustrated, stylised diagram showing a chaotic digital prescription workflow. The background resembles a tablet or screen filled with tangled pathways, pop-up messages, and warning labels. posts.caption:
A Proud Moment for Titan PMR – EPS is Now Fully Rolled Out Across Wales

On 17th November 2023, Titan PMR made history.

We became the first PMR to deploy EPS in Wales, with Wellington Road Pharmacy in Rhyl leading the way.

Patients there were the first in Wales to experience a fully digital prescription process.

Now, EPS is fully rolled out across all Titan pharmacies in Wales, marking a major milestone in our mission to transform pharmacy care.

What does this mean for pharmacies?

✅ No more paper prescriptions → Fully digital workflow  

✅ Faster, safer dispensing → Fewer errors, better stock control  

✅ Less admin, more efficiency → Simplified reimbursements and operations

# Short Clip Transcript – Andy Testimonial

"Would you go back to your old PMR system? Never, surely, absolutely. Pharmacists are only having to be involved in about 20% of the accuracy checks, and with Titan X, their involvement in the clinical checks is assisted as well. We needed to move away from the pharmacist being bogged down with checking every single prescription. We needed to free up their time so that they could engage with all the new NHS priorities, which is where all the funding is at. Pharmacists have noticed the difference almost straight away. The dispensers are empowered to do a lot more in terms of their roles. They can complete a lot more prescriptions. Such an innovative platform, one of the things I've noticed has made a real difference in one of my shops is the way it manages electronic repeat dispensing. It will automatically schedule downloads every four weeks, or you can edit that to change the interval if need be you can pull them down if you need them earlier. Really flexible, but it means that we aren't taking hundreds of phone calls a month about electronic repeat dispensary prescriptions that need to be pulled down and dispensed. We can just tell patients to relax and come in when they're running out. So make a difference Absolutely. Yeah, it reduced the phone call significantly. Yeah."

• More consultations, more revenue.  

• Faster, smarter patient care.  

• A future-ready pharmacy that thrives under pressure.

posts.alt_text:
# TITAN's Latest Update: Supporting Original Pack Dispensing Legislation

As the pharmacy industry prepares for significant regulatory changes, TITAN continues to lead the way with innovation and adaptability. With the Original Pack Dispensing (OPD) legislation coming into effect from 1st January 2025, TITAN is proud to announce full support for this game-changing update, ensuring pharmacies stay compliant while optimising their operations.

## What is Original Pack Dispensing?

From January 2025, the new OPD legislation mandates that pharmacists must dispense medication in its original manufacturer's pack when clinically appropriate. This change aims to:

- Reduce medicine waste  

- Minimise split packs  

- Enhance dispensing accuracy  

This regulation simplifies processes for pharmacies and offers a better experience for patients, aligning with broader NHS goals of efficiency and safety.

## TITAN's Seamless Integration of OPD

TITAN has seamlessly incorporated OPD functionality into its platform, making the transition smooth and efficient for pharmacies. Here's how it works:

### Effortless Workflow

Integrated directly into the labelling screen, OPD requires no additional training or steps. Simply scan the product, and TITAN automatically calculates the pack difference for the pharmacist.

### Automation at Its Best

TITAN's advanced logic ensures accuracy by identifying the most appropriate pack sizes, reducing manual interventions.

## Key Benefits

- Reduction in Waste: Dispensing full packs eliminates unnecessary splitting.  

- Improved Accuracy: TITAN's automation minimises errors during dispensing.  

- Operational Efficiency: Pharmacies save time while meeting regulatory compliance.  

## Future-Ready with TITAN

With the OPD update, TITAN once again proves its commitment to innovation and compliance. As the NHS-accredited platform leading pharmacy transformation, TITAN ensures pharmacists can focus on clinical care while leaving regulatory changes to the experts.

Stay ahead of the curve with TITAN — a partner you can trust to adapt to the evolving pharmacy landscape.

For more details on Original Pack Dispensing visit CPE - Original Pack Dispensing.
